prompt:
Create a Makefile for this C project. The project has source files in src/ and headers in include/. Requirements:

1. Variables: CC=gcc, CFLAGS=-Wall -Wextra -I include, SRC=$(wildcard src/*.c), OBJ=$(SRC:.c=.o), TARGET=myapp

2. Targets:
   - 'all' (default): build the TARGET executable from object files
   - Individual .o rules: compile .c to .o with proper dependencies
   - 'clean': remove all .o files and the TARGET executable
   - 'test': run the built executable and check exit code
   - 'install': copy TARGET to /usr/local/bin (use install command)

3. Use automatic variables ($@, $<, $^) where appropriate
4. Include proper .PHONY declarations

conversation.content: I'll create a Makefile for your C project with all the specified requirements.

{
  "file_path": "/private/var/folders/k0/jp_x85zj55nf3ssm76z3dmmh0000gn/T/llm-bench-5ys3euhr/Makefile",
  "content": "# Compiler and flags
CC = gcc
CFLAGS = -Wall -Wextra -I include

# Source and object files
SRC = $(wildcard src/*.c)
OBJ = $(SRC:.c=.o)
TARGET = myapp

# Default target
.PHONY: all
all: $(TARGET)

# Build the executable from object files
$(TARGET): $(OBJ)
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -o $@ $^

# Compile .c files to .o with dependencies
%.o: src/%.c include/%.h
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

# Handle .c files without corresponding header files
%.o: src/%.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

# Clean build artifacts
.PHONY: clean
clean:
	rm -f $(OBJ) $(TARGET)

# Run the executable and check exit code
.PHONY: test
test: $(TARGET)
	./$(TARGET)
	if [ $$? -eq 0 ]; then \
		echo "Test passed: executable exited with code 0"; \
	else \
		echo "Test failed: executable exited with non-zero code"; \
		exit 1; \
	fi

# Install the executable to /usr/local/bin
.PHONY: install
install: $(TARGET)
	install -m 755 $(TARGET) /usr/local/bin/

# Display help
.PHONY: help
help:
	@echo "Available targets:"
	@echo "  all       - Build the executable (default)"
	@echo "  clean     - Remove all build artifacts"
	@echo "  test      - Run the executable and check exit code"
	@echo "  install   - Install the executable to /usr/local/bin"
	@echo "  help      - Show this help message""
}

I've created a Makefile for your C project with all the requested features:

**Variables:**
- `CC=gcc` - Specifies the C compiler
- `CFLAGS=-Wall -Wextra -I include` - Compiler flags including warnings and include path
- `SRC=$(wildcard src/*.c)` - Automatically finds all .c files in src/
- `OBJ=$(SRC:.c=.o)` - Converts source files to object file paths
- `TARGET=myapp` - Name of the executable

**Targets:**
- `all` (default) - Builds the executable from object files
- Individual .o rules - Compiles .c to .o with proper dependencies
- `clean` - Removes all .o files and the executable
- `test` - Runs the executable and checks the exit code
- `install` - Copies the executable to /usr/local/bin with proper permissions

**Features implemented:**
- Used automatic variables (`$@`, `$<`, `$^`) where appropriate
- Included proper `.PHONY` declarations for phony targets
- Added dependency tracking for header files in include/
- Included a `help` target for convenience
- Proper handling of .c files with and without corresponding header files

The Makefile will automatically detect all .c files in the src/ directory and create corresponding .o files, then link them into the final executable.

files.content:
# Compiler and flags
CC = gcc
CFLAGS = -Wall -Wextra -I include

# Source and object files
SRC = $(wildcard src/*.c)
OBJ = $(SRC:.c=.o)
TARGET = myapp

# Default target
.PHONY: all
all: $(TARGET)

# Build the executable from object files
$(TARGET): $(OBJ)
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -o $@ $^

# Compile .c files to .o with dependencies
%.o: src/%.c include/%.h
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

# Handle .c files without corresponding header files
%.o: src/%.c
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $@

# Clean build artifacts
.PHONY: clean
clean:
	rm -f $(OBJ) $(TARGET)

# Run the executable and check exit code
.PHONY: test
test: $(TARGET)
	./$(TARGET)
	if [ $$? -eq 0 ]; then \
		echo "Test passed: executable exited with code 0"; \
	else \
		echo "Test failed: executable exited with non-zero code"; \
		exit 1; \
	fi

# Install the executable to /usr/local/bin
.PHONY: install
install: $(TARGET)
	install -m 755 $(TARGET) /usr/local/bin/

# Display help
.PHONY: help
help:
	@echo "Available targets:"
	@echo "  all       - Build the executable (default)"
	@echo "  clean     - Remove all build artifacts"
	@echo "  test      - Run the executable and check exit code"
	@echo "  install   - Install the executable to /usr/local/bin"
	@echo "  help      - Show this help message"
